content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 7, 2000


Tim Fleming
RMC Pacific Materials
6601 Koll Center Parkway
Pleasanton, California 94566

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-00-043

Dear Mr. Fleming:

This letter is in response to your e-mail request for general assistance regarding the
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Because your questions are general in nature
and do not pertain to a specific governmental decision, we are treating your request as one for
general assistance.²

You are inquiring regarding the laws pertaining to contributions made by corporations
and unions in California. You also ask what guidelines local county and city campaigns fall
under.

Corporations and unions are not currently prohibited from making contributions in
California under the Act. However, local jurisdictions may have restrictions on corporate or
union contributions. You would need to check with the particular county or city to determine if
there are restrictions.

In addition, you may want to check with the Federal Election Commission regarding
federal law and its rules regarding corporate and union contributions in California.

We may not advise you about other laws outside of the Act.

___________________________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91015. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18996, of the California Code of Regulations.

² General assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided in an opinion or formal
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c).)

File No. G-00-043
Page 2

City and county jurisdictions may adopt their own ordinances or rules concerning
contribution limits and other matters unless the rules conflict with the Act. (Section 81013).
Again, you would have to check with the city or county regarding this particular question.

I have attached our Campaign Manuals A and E, along with the 2000 Addendum. Please
note that page 38 of Manual E states that corporations may make political contributions in
California. In addition, page 89 of Manual A contains information about local limits.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
Assistant General Counsel

By: Lynda Doherty
Political Reform Consultant, Legal Division

LM:LD:tls

Enclosures

I:\Advice Ltrs\00043.wpd

sections.question: You are inquiring regarding the laws pertaining to contributions made by corporations and unions in California. You also ask what guidelines local county and city campaigns fall under.
sections.conclusion: Corporations and unions are not currently prohibited from making contributions in California under the Act. However, local jurisdictions may have restrictions on corporate or union contributions. You would need to check with the particular county or city to determine if there are restrictions. City and county jurisdictions may adopt their own ordinances or rules concerning contribution limits and other matters unless the rules conflict with the Act.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the legal restrictions on political contributions by corporations and unions in California, and what guidelines apply to local county and city campaigns?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Under California law, corporations and unions may make political contributions, but local jurisdictions can impose their own restrictions on such contributions provided they do not conflict with state law.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document treated as general assistance request rather than formal written advice. No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections with headers, but question and conclusion content are clearly identifiable in the body text. File number G-00-043 confirmed.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You are inquiring regarding the laws pertaining to contributions made by corporations and unions in California. You also ask what guidelines local county and city campaigns fall under.

CONCLUSION: Corporations and unions are not currently prohibited from making contributions in California under the Act. However, local jurisdictions may have restrictions on corporate or union contributions. You would need to check with the particular county or city to determine if there are restrictions. City and county jurisdictions may adopt their own ordinances or rules concerning contribution limits and other matters unless the rules conflict with the Act.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that corporations and unions are permitted to make political contributions under California's Political Reform Act, but notes that local jurisdictions may impose additional restrictions and recommends the requestor check with specific counties or cities for local requirements.
